claims.subject: Deborah
claims.object: teaches yoga classes to community and neighbors
claims.retrieval_text: Deborah teaches yoga. Deborah is a yoga instructor who teaches classes in the park and neighborhood. She is passionate about sharing yoga with the community.

claims.subject: Deborah
claims.object: mother passed away a few years ago
claims.retrieval_text: Deborah's mother passed away. Deborah visits her mother's old house and sits on a special bench by the window to feel connected to her.

claims.subject: Deborah
claims.object: father passed away on January 25, 2023
claims.retrieval_text: Deborah's father died suddenly two days before January 27, 2023. His sudden death left the family shell-shocked.

claims.subject: Deborah
claims.object: finds peace and comfort in nature and memories
claims.retrieval_text: Deborah finds peace in nature and memories. She visits her mother's house, sits on the bench, goes to parks, beaches, and gardens to find comfort and reflect.

claims.retrieval_text: Deborah is married. Her husband appears in photos. They value love and openness. She got married on a beach. They play detective games together.

claims.retrieval_text: Deborah's parents' wedding was in 1993. She looked at the family album after her father's death.

claims.retrieval_text: Deborah has a pendant or amulet from her mother. She brings it when visiting reflective spots. It provides comfort and connection to her late mother.

claims.retrieval_text: Deborah lost her friend Karlie. Their last photo was from a hike the previous summer. Deborah spent time in a garden with roses and dahlias for comfort.

claims.retrieval_text: Deborah visited Bali last year and did yoga on the beach. She described it as gorgeous and peaceful.

claims.retrieval_text: Deborah visited Rio de Janeiro three years ago and took photos on excursions.

claims.object: likes cats, does not like dogs
claims.retrieval_text: Deborah likes cats and does not like dogs. She has two cats: Luna (5 years old, from shelter) and Max (8 years old, inherited from her mother).

claims.retrieval_text: Deborah started a running group with her neighbor Anna. They support and motivate each other during runs.

claims.retrieval_text: Deborah surfs and does yoga on the beach. She loves the ocean, sand, and fresh air.

claims.retrieval_text: Deborah's mother's old house has a special bench by the window. Deborah visits regularly to sit there, do yoga, and feel connected to her mother. There is also a bench/spot by the water nearby.

claims.subject: Jolene
claims.object: electrical engineering student
claims.retrieval_text: Jolene is an electrical engineering student. She works on robotics projects, built a sustainable water purifier, and designs aerial surveillance prototypes.

claims.retrieval_text: Jolene's mother also passed away last year. She has memories of her mother's house and room.

claims.retrieval_text: Jolene has a pendant from her mother given in 2010 in Paris. It has a symbol representing freedom. It reminds her to pursue her goals.

claims.retrieval_text: Jolene has two pet snakes: Susie (adopted two years ago when feeling lonely) and Seraphim (bought in Paris a year ago). They bring her comfort and calm.

claims.retrieval_text: Jolene loves video games. She learned to play on her own as a child. Her parents got her first console at age 10. She plays with her partner regularly.

claims.retrieval_text: Jolene has a boyfriend/partner. They have been together for three years and met in an engineering class in college. They are not married.

claims.subject: Jolene
claims.object: built sustainable water purifier for rural community
claims.retrieval_text: Jolene designed and built a sustainable water purifier for a rural community in need. Seeing it provide clean water was incredibly satisfying.

claims.subject: Jolene
claims.object: use engineering to make positive impact on communities in need
claims.retrieval_text: Jolene wants to use engineering to make a positive impact. She is interested in green tech, renewable energy (solar), clean water access, and sustainable solutions for disadvantaged areas.

claims.retrieval_text: Jolene proposed a volunteer program where engineers teach STEM to underprivileged kids. She planned to partner with local schools and invite guest speakers.

claims.retrieval_text: Jolene did a mini retreat last Wednesday to assess where she is in life. It gave her a new outlook and confidence boost.

claims.retrieval_text: Jolene uses the Pomodoro Technique (25 minutes work, 5-minute break) to avoid burnout while studying.

claims.retrieval_text: Jolene started interning at a well-known engineering firm. She applies what she learned in school to real projects.

claims.retrieval_text: Jolene has been doing yoga sporadically for about 3 years. It helps her escape studying and work stress.

claims.retrieval_text: Jolene's favorite yoga pose is savasana (corpse pose). She finds it calming and it helps her let go.

claims.retrieval_text: Jolene had a huge setback when her project crashed and she lost all her work files. It was frustrating and depressing. Meditation helped her cope.

claims.retrieval_text: Jolene started using a bullet journal for tracking tasks and staying organized. She finds crossing off tasks satisfying.
